methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $315M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sunfire, a Series E climate and energy technology company that has raised $315M+, operates within a competitive frontier-tech landscape spanning 1 public comparable (Lanza Technologies) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. The narrowness of direct valuation comparables at Sunfire's stage and valuation tier constrains traditional peer analysis.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.4x multiplier to the $315M total capital raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $441M. This multiplier reflects typical step-up dynamics from Series E to subsequent funding rounds. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable; while 19 same-sector companies exist, insufficient narrow-stage and similar-tier comparables (fewer than 3 datapoints meeting strict filters) preclude reliable median-based triangulation without risking distortion from broader sector medians.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $375M to $507M, with a median point estimate of $441M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty around downstream round dynamics and the appropriate step-up multiple for this stage. Key risks to the valuation include: refinement of the 1.4x multiplier based on actual Series E→F precedents in adjacent frontier-energy subsectors, and resolution of technology-to-commercialization risk, which may compress or expand multiples depending on proof points achieved between valuation date and next capital event.
